executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 100+ organizations compromised in a single 48-hour period. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (database leaked), and the Municipality of North Perth. Critical infrastructure sectors affected include healthcare (multiple hospitals and clinics), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Collège Supérieur de Montréal), public health (Peterborough Public Health), and financial services. The Qilin ransomware group is the most active threat actor with 18 confirmed Canadian victims, followed by Play (9 victims), Safepay (11 victims), and Clop (13 victims).

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is experiencing a catastrophic ransomware surge affecting 100+ organizations across all critical sectors within 48 hours. Healthcare institutions under active attack include Markham Stouffville Hospital, Peterborough Public Health, Thunder Bay Counselling, and multiple medical clinics. Public sector compromises include Municipality of North Perth, Eastern Townships School Board, and Workers Health & Safety Centre. No CCCS advisories were issued during this period despite the crisis-level incident volume.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose compromised by two separate ransomware groups (Coinbasecartel and Shinyhunters), Active Green + Ross hit by Sinobi, Kids & Company breached by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered database leak via Everest ransomware, Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi, multiple accounting firms breached including Pewarchuk CPA and MG Chartered Professional Accountant

report_2w.executive_summary: CISA has added Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog with a federal deadline of April 30, indicating active exploitation in the wild. Separately, the Qilin ransomware group has claimed multiple new victims including Nanometrics, Henley, and The Great Cookie, while ShinyHunters claims an 80-million-record breach of Rockstar Games via supply chain attack and an 8.7-million-record theft from Carnival Corporation. Iran-linked CyberAv3ngers (IRGC) are actively targeting Rockwell PLCs across U.S. critical infrastructure. A critical remote code execution vulnerability has been patched in the widely-used Protobuf.js library (NPM), and Amtrak customer data including 2.1 million emails has been leaked following a Salesforce environment compromise.

items.description: Apache ActiveMQ vulnerability added to CISA KEV catalog
items.cia_impact: Unknown - exploitation confirmed, full impact details not provided

items.description: Critical remote code execution vulnerability in Protobuf.js library (NPM)
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High (RCE)

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Rockstar Games (80 million records ransomed and leaked), Carnival Corporation (8.7 million records), Vercel platform breach, Amtrak/Salesforce (2.1 million emails)
items.ttps: T1195.002 - Supply Chain Compromise (Rockstar Games attack), T1078 - Valid Accounts (Salesforce environment compromise), T1567 - Exfiltration Over Web Service, T1486 - Data Encrypted for Impact (ransom demand)

items.name: Qilin (ransomware group)
items.targets: Nanometrics, Henley, The Great Cookie, ongoing impact to NHS Trust from 2024 attack
items.ttps: T1486 - Data Encrypted for Impact, T1491 - Defacement (dark web leak site posts), T1567 - Exfiltration Over Web Service

items.name: CyberAv3ngers (Iran-linked IRGC)
items.targets: Rockwell PLCs across U.S. critical infrastructure
items.ttps: T1584 - Compromise Infrastructure, T1588.006 - Obtain Capabilities: Vulnerabilities, T1200 - Hardware Additions (PLC targeting)

items.name: JanelaRAT operators
items.ttps: T1566.001 - Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment, T1204 - User Execution, T1056.001 - Input Capture: Keylogging (banking trojan)

items.name: Agent Tesla operators
items.ttps: T1566.001 - Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment, T1056.001 - Input Capture: Keylogging, T1113 - Screen Capture

canada_landscape.summary: No Canadian-specific cybersecurity incidents or CCCS advisories were identified in the reporting period. Multiple articles mentioned Canada in non-cybersecurity contexts (transport surveys, sports, politics, immigration, general news). No breaches affecting major Canadian retailers (Loblaws, Canadian Tire, Shoppers Drug Mart) or financial institutions (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO) were reported.
